SEARCH
Package edu.bu.pas.uno.tree

Class Node

java.lang.Object
edu.bu.pas.uno.tree.Node
public abstract class Node extends Object
A class for an arbitrary node in the game tree. To help with the complexity of the game I have created some nested classes to index the cases when the current player has no legal moves to do. These cases correspond to values of the Node.NodeState enum, and their individual indices into the q-values are told via the class DrawUnresolvedCardsIdxs and DrawSingleCardIdxs. When the current player has legal moves there will be one q-value allocated per legal move. When the current player is forced to draw a single card there will be two q-values: one for the player keeping the card and another for the player playing the card (if it is legal to play). If the player is forced to draw a bunch of cards due to unresolved "Draw XX" cards there will be one q-value.
See Also:
  • Node.NodeState
  • Node.NoLegalMovesIdxDefaults.DrawUnresolvedCardsIdxs
  • Node.NoLegalMovesIdxDefaults.DrawSingleCardIdxs

  • Method Details

    • getGameView

      public final Game.GameView getGameView()

    • getDepth

      public final int getDepth()

    • getLogicalPlayerIdx

      public final int getLogicalPlayerIdx()

    • getParent

      public final Node getParent()

    • getOrderedLegalMoves

      public final List<Integer> getOrderedLegalMoves()
      A getter method to get the list of legal moves the current player can make. Only use if the state of the Node is HAS_LEGAL_MOVES. If the state of the Node is anything else this list will be empty and will not correspond to the q-values.
      Returns:
      The list of legal moves (that is only populated if the player has legal moves available).

    • getNodeState

      public final Node.NodeState getNodeState()

    • isTerminal

      public final boolean isTerminal()

    • getStateCount

      public final long getStateCount()

    • getQValueTotal

      public final float getQValueTotal(int moveIdx)

    • getQCount

      public final long getQCount(int moveIdx)

    • setQValueTotal

      public final void setQValueTotal(int moveIdx, float qValueTotal)

    • setQCount

      public final void setQCount(int moveIdx, long qCount)

    • getQValue

      public final float getQValue(int moveIdx)

    • getUtilityValues

      public final float getUtilityValues()
      A getter method to get the utility value of the Game.GameView inside this Node. The utility value is calculated as the expected value of the q-values (e.g. weighted by the frequencies of the q-values).
      Returns:
      The utility value.

    • getChild

      public abstract Node getChild(Move move)
